name: PerpetualsMarket

args.name: oracle_app
args.desc: Oracle application ID for reading price

args.name: perps_pool_app
args.desc: Perpetuals pool application ID for PnL calls

methods.name: open_position

args.name: is_long
args.desc: True for long, False for short

methods.desc:
Open a perpetual position.
Margin must be sent as ALGO payment in group transaction.

returns.desc: Amount returned to trader (margin +/- PnL - fees)

methods.name: add_margin
methods.desc:
Add margin to an existing position.
ALGO must be sent in group transaction.

methods.name: liquidate
methods.desc:
Liquidate an underwater position.
Anyone can call this to liquidate positions below maintenance margin. Liquidator receives a reward.

methods.name: apply_funding
methods.desc:
Apply accumulated funding to a position.
Can be called by anyone (keeper bot).

methods.name: is_liquidatable
methods.desc: Check if a position is liquidatable.

methods.name: get_current_price
methods.desc: Get current ALGO price from oracle.

methods.name: calculate_required_margin
methods.desc: Calculate required margin for a position.

methods.name: calculate_liquidation_price_view
methods.desc: Calculate liquidation price for a position.

methods.name: update_pool_app_id
methods.desc: Update pool application ID (for contract upgrades).

methods.name: get_current_fee_split
methods.desc: Get current fee split percentages based on contract health.

args.name: min_operational
args.desc: Minimum balance before critical mode (default 1 ALGO)

args.name: reserve_target
args.desc: Target balance for healthy mode (default 10 ALGO)

desc:
Perpetual futures market contract.

    Enables leveraged trading on ALGO price with no expiry.
    Pool acts as counterparty to all positions.

    Position Mechanics:
    - Long: Profit when price increases, loss when decreases
    - Short: Profit when price decreases, loss when increases
    - Leverage amplifies both profits and losses

    Liquidation:
    - Triggered when margin ratio falls below maintenance margin
    - Liquidator receives reward (percentage of remaining margin)
    - Remaining margin goes to pool

    Funding:
    - Paid every hour
    - Rate determined by OI imbalance
    - Incentivizes balanced market
